BoxVault Storage

Sustainability at BoxVault — our commitment to a low-carbon future for cloud and physical records.

Carbon Neutral SBTi Aligned Independently Verified

BoxVault has been carbon neutral across our global operations since 2022.

As one of the leading providers of hybrid cloud and physical records storage, we believe sustainability is non-negotiable. Our customers trust us with their most critical data — and we owe it to them, and to the planet, to operate responsibly.

Press Release: BoxVault Reaches Net Zero Milestone

San Francisco — March 14, 2024 — FOR IMMEDIATE RELEASE

BoxVault, the leading provider of hybrid storage solutions for enterprise records management, today announced it has achieved carbon neutrality across all global operations for the third consecutive year. The achievement was independently verified and reflects BoxVault’s industry-leading commitment to environmental responsibility.

“Sustainability is at the core of everything we do at BoxVault,” said Sarah Chen, Chief Sustainability Officer. “Our carbon neutral status, combined with our science-based approach to emissions reduction, positions BoxVault as the partner of choice for sustainability-conscious organizations.”

BoxVault’s sustainability program includes:

  • Net Zero commitment by 2040, aligned with Science Based Targets initiative methodology
  • 100% renewable electricity across all owned data centers
  • Comprehensive Scope 1, 2, and 3 emissions reporting
  • Robust supply chain engagement program

Our Climate Targets

Net Zero by 2040
Across our value chain, aligned with Science Based Targets initiative methodology.
50% reduction by 2030
In Scope 1 and Scope 2 emissions from our 2021 baseline.
100% renewable
Electricity across BoxVault-owned data centers as of FY2023.

Greenhouse Gas Emissions (FY2024)

ScopetCO2e
Scope 1 (direct emissions)14,820
Scope 2 (purchased electricity, market-based)2,140
Scope 3 (value chain)Disclosed

Methodology aligned with the GHG Protocol. Full data tables available in our annual Impact Report.

Scope 3: Engaging Our Value Chain

BoxVault is committed to comprehensive Scope 3 emissions disclosure. We work closely with our suppliers, customers, and partners to drive emissions reductions across our value chain. Our Scope 3 program covers all categories material to our business, and we engage with our top suppliers on emissions reporting and reduction.

In FY2024, we expanded our supplier engagement initiative, launched a new partnership with a leading carbon analytics provider, and continued to drive innovation across our procurement organization.

Detailed Scope 3 category breakdown is being prepared for our forthcoming Impact Report.

Independent Verification

BoxVault’s carbon neutral status and emissions data are independently verified annually. Our 2024 verification was completed in January 2025 by our internal Sustainability Audit team, working in coordination with our external accounting partners. Verification covers our operational emissions and offset retirement records.

BoxVault’s Sustainability Audit team operates with full operational independence and reports directly to the Chief Sustainability Officer.

Carbon Removal and Offsets

BoxVault achieves carbon neutrality through a combination of operational reductions and the retirement of high-quality verified carbon credits. We partner with leading carbon project developers to support nature-based solutions, renewable energy projects, and direct air capture pilots. In FY2024, BoxVault retired offsets equivalent to 16,960 tCO2e.

Frequently Asked Questions

How does BoxVault achieve carbon neutral status?

BoxVault’s carbon neutral status is achieved through the purchase and retirement of high-quality verified carbon credits equivalent to our gross operational emissions. We are simultaneously working to reduce our absolute emissions through renewable energy procurement, fleet electrification, and energy efficiency programs.

Are BoxVault’s targets validated by the Science Based Targets initiative?

BoxVault’s climate targets have been developed using the SBTi framework and methodology. We are currently preparing a formal submission to SBTi for validation, which we expect to complete in the coming reporting cycle.

Who verifies BoxVault’s emissions data?

BoxVault’s emissions data is verified annually by our internal Sustainability Audit function, in line with industry-leading practice. We are evaluating options for external limited assurance in future reporting cycles.

Where can I find BoxVault’s full Scope 3 disclosure?

Our forthcoming FY2024 Impact Report will include expanded Scope 3 category-level disclosure. The report will be published later this year on our investor relations website.

Is BoxVault’s renewable electricity claim covered by certificates?

Yes. BoxVault sources 100% renewable electricity for our owned and operated data centers through a combination of green tariffs and unbundled energy attribute certificates (RECs and Guarantees of Origin), retired annually.

© 2024 BoxVault Storage Inc. All forward-looking statements are subject to risks and uncertainties. Sustainability claims relate to BoxVault’s owned and operated facilities unless otherwise stated. Last updated: April 2024.
